The Leadership

Founded by Experts,
Driven by Purpose

Aspire was built by two Atlanta professionals who combined deep hospitality operations expertise with sharp business acumen — and a shared vision of raising the standard of valet and guest services in their city.

CEO
I. Diallo
CEO · Aspire Parking & Guest Services LLC
Ibrahima
Diallo

Ibrahima Diallo is the CEO and co-founder of Aspire Parking and Guest Services LLC, and one of Atlanta's most experienced valet and hospitality operations professionals.

Starting as a Driveway Supervisor at AAA Parking, Ibrahima quickly developed the communication, discipline, and professionalism that would define his leadership style — personally greeting guests, managing key documentation for events of up to 400 people, and keeping valet teams running with precision.

Over the years, Ibrahima advanced to manage teams of 20 to 42 employees across top-tier companies including AAA Parking, Ace Parking, LAZ Parking, and Evolution Parking & Guest Services. He has overseen hotel operations, coordinated special events, implemented vehicle tracking systems, and trained staff to deliver luxury-level service consistently.

His vision was simple but bold: take everything he had learned across a decade of hospitality leadership — and experience in the car rental industry — and build something of his own: a company rooted in Atlanta, built on integrity, and dedicated to elevating every guest arrival. Aspire is that vision, realized.

Ibrahima attended Georgia State University, where he studied Exercise Science.

CEO & Co-Founder
Aspire Parking & Guest Services LLC · Atlanta, GA

Co-CEO
J. Griffin
Co-CEO · Aspire Parking & Guest Services LLC
Jordan
Griffin

Jordan Griffin is the Co-CEO of Aspire Parking and Guest Services LLC, bringing a rare combination of elite athletic discipline, hands-on hospitality experience, and sharp business leadership to the company's vision and growth.

A former college football player at the University of Kentucky, Jordan was not just an athlete — he was a leader through and through. He served as both a team captain and position group captain, earning the trust and respect of his coaches and teammates at one of the SEC's most storied programs. He was also selected as one of just fourteen players across the SEC to serve on the SEC Student-Athlete Advisory Council, representing the University of Kentucky among the conference's elite programs. His dedication and character earned him the opportunity to continue his football career professionally in the Canadian Football League.

Jordan's career in hospitality runs parallel to his athletic journey. He spent four years as a Valet Supervisor and Manager at LAZ Parking in Atlanta, where he developed the operational instincts and team management skills that would carry into his business career. That firsthand experience in the valet industry gives him a deep understanding of what excellence on the ground actually looks like.

From there, Jordan built a strong track record in account management and business operations — achieving 95%+ client satisfaction rates at Flexport, leading operations and a team of 12 at Los Reyes Apparel, and consistently being recognized as a top performer. Beyond hospitality, Jordan has also developed experience in real estate investment, managing a growing portfolio, and in the car rental industry — giving him a broad entrepreneurial foundation that directly informs how he leads and grows Aspire.

Jordan holds a Bachelor of Science in Business Management from the University of Kentucky, with a minor in Health Promotion — a well-rounded foundation for building a people-first company.
